title: galium album subsp. suberectum
intro: Galium album subsp. suberectum, also known as the upright bedstraw, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Galium album subsp. suberectum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and support the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or using them as a tincture, although further research is necessary to fully understand its uses and effects.
